entries.title: Floor 1: Guild Forward Base
entries.summary: The guild has pushed a small forward base into the first floor of Moonroot Hollow. Lantern racks, bedrolls, chalk marks, and barricades turn the entry rooms into a working lesson for new delvers.
notes: Guild rooms are bright and mostly safe.
North of the training barricades, the Hollow starts answering back.
The mapper's chalk is the party's best early warning system.

entries.title: Quartermaster Sella
entries.summary: Sella keeps the base supplied and measures courage by how many practical things a delver remembers to buy before leaving lamplight.
notes: Runs the item shop on Floor 1.
Her shelves mark the safest edge of guild control.

entries.title: Guild Mapper
entries.summary: The mapper teaches new delvers to trust marked paths, distrust empty stone, and write down what the dungeon tries to make them forget.
notes: Chalk marks signal routes where someone returned with steady hands.

entries.title: Cook Marlo
entries.summary: Marlo keeps a low cookfire burning at the edge of the base, turning food into courage before the doors make courage expensive.
notes: Runs the food shop on Floor 1.
Dungeon meat should be cooked longer than pride thinks necessary.

entries.title: Wounded Delver
entries.summary: A hurt delver in the infirmary has already paid for several lessons the party can learn more cheaply by listening.
notes: Warns that safety becomes a story past the chalk fork.
Proof that the first floor is still a dungeon.

entries.title: Guild Rookie
entries.summary: The rookie repeats the rules with the nervous precision of someone who has decided the rules might keep all their toes attached.
notes: Points rookies toward the marked practice plates.
A reminder that every veteran was new once.

entries.title: Descent Guard
entries.summary: The guard watches the stairs down with a veteran's patience and an exact sense for when a party has not yet learned enough.
notes: Blocks the lesson from becoming a funeral march.
The second floor is beyond routine guild control.

entries.title: Practice Plate
entries.summary: Guild-safe pressure plates teach boot discipline before the Hollow's real traps start collecting mistakes.
entries.effect: Armed for instruction rather than injury.
entries.counter: Use marked plates to learn the shape of a trap before trusting unmarked stone.

entries.title: Spike Plate
entries.summary: Spring-driven spikes punish careless steps and clustered movement.
entries.counter: Watch for regular plates, scratches, and repeated stone seams.

entries.title: Dart Line
entries.summary: Wall seams can hide dart launchers aimed at the lead delver.
entries.counter: Let the scout check long straight corridors and suspicious wall slits.

entries.title: Poison Mist
entries.summary: Old vents exhale toxic mist into rooms that look safer than they are.
entries.counter: Carry antidotes and avoid lingering near cracked floor vents.

entries.title: Alarm Rune
entries.summary: A glowing rune that wakes nearby creatures and turns quiet rooms loud.
entries.counter: Treat warm glyphs and clean circular marks as live threats.

entries.title: Crumbling Bridge
entries.summary: False stone and cracked bridges hold just long enough to be believed.
entries.effect: Damages the party when the floor gives way.
entries.counter: Cross slowly when stone color changes or support roots look hollow.

entries.title: Teleport Snare
entries.summary: Moonroot snares fold a party across the floor without asking where the plan went.
entries.effect: Moves the party to another position on the floor.
entries.counter: Avoid blue circular traces and rooms with too much untouched dust.

entries.title: Pitfall
entries.summary: Rare false floors drop delvers deeper, but the Hollow will not skip an unbeaten boss gate.
entries.effect: Drops the party one to three floors, capped by uncleared boss floors.
entries.counter: Probe isolated floor panels and never sprint over new darkness.

entries.title: Goblins
entries.summary: Moonroot goblins move in scavenger packs, prize useful scrap, and adapt quickly to guild habits. Scouts test paths first, grunts hold ground, and trappers turn loose wire into bad news.
entries.weakness: Disrupt their formation before the clever one gets time to prepare.

entries.title: Training Slime
entries.summary: A soft-bodied lesson creature used to teach basic battle rhythm before the Hollow raises the stakes.

entries.title: Thorn Imp
entries.summary: A quick thorn-backed nuisance that favors sharp lunges and ugly timing.

entries.title: Road Wisp
entries.summary: A drifting spirit-light that lashes out with small arcs of magic.

entries.title: Bramble Rat
entries.summary: A fast, mean scavenger that treats ankles as invitations.
entries.weakness: Predictable once forced into a straight fight.

entries.title: Moonroot Bat
entries.summary: A crystal-touched cave bat that reads air currents better than most delvers read maps.
entries.weakness: Low defense; punish it when it dives.

entries.title: Goblin Scout
entries.summary: A quick first-contact goblin that tests weak lines and runs toward useful noise.

entries.title: Goblin Grunt
entries.summary: A tougher goblin that makes up for poor finesse with a shield, a club, and complete confidence in both.
entries.weakness: Slower than scouts and vulnerable to being outmaneuvered.

entries.title: Goblin Trapper
entries.summary: A specialist goblin carrying wire, darts, bait, and too many ideas.
entries.weakness: Interrupt it before tools become terrain.

entries.title: Hollow Guard
entries.summary: A stone-heavy defender shaped by the dungeon's deeper pressure.
entries.weakness: Slow and easier to manage with guarded turns.

entries.title: Crystal Ooze
entries.summary: An ooze threaded with blue crystal that stores enough magic to make poking it feel like a debate.
entries.weakness: Slow; strike before it gathers force.

entries.title: Vorthane, Warden of the Backroom
entries.summary: The first great gatekeeper of Moonroot Hollow, heavy with old authority and root-cracked power.
entries.weakness: Long fights favor preparation, guarding, and steady healing.

entries.title: Deep Moonroot Bat
entries.summary: A deeper bat grown meaner, faster, and brighter around the crystals.

entries.title: Gloom Ooze
entries.summary: A darker ooze that turns the floor's dimness into a weapon.

entries.title: Rootbound Guard
entries.summary: A hardened guard anchored by roots and built to make retreat look sensible.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 1
entries.summary: A guild-held forward base that teaches the language of doors, chalk, traps, and goblin signs.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 2
entries.summary: The guild's lesson ends and live dungeon stone begins: cisterns, archives, alarms, and hidden pressure plates.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 3
entries.summary: A contested floor of dim roots, poison vents, and unstable paths.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 4
entries.summary: A floor where wall seams and dart lines start to matter more than bravery.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 5
entries.summary: The Hollow grows trickier here, mixing alarms, teleport snares, and crystal oozes.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 6
entries.summary: A damp, hostile stretch of poison, alarms, and paths that dislike staying where they were.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 7
entries.summary: A darker floor where spikes and snares make clean routes feel earned.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 8
entries.summary: Pitfalls join the lesson, and the Hollow starts testing how carefully the party remembers boss gates.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 9
entries.summary: The last stretch before the first warden, thick with collapsing stone and hard guards.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 10
entries.summary: A boss floor built around Vorthane's threshold. The descent does not continue until the warden falls.

entries.title: Moonroot Hollow - Floor 11
entries.summary: Beyond the first gate, the dungeon grows deeper, darker, and less interested in warning anyone twice.
